summary.text: Fundamental and historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP.

overview.description: Meta Platforms, Inc. engages in the development of products that enable people to connect and share with friends and family through mobile devices, personal computers, virtual reality (VR) headsets, and AI glasses in the United States, Canada, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and internationally. It operates through two segments, Family of Apps (FoA) and Reality Labs (RL). The FoA segment offers Facebook, which enables people to build community through feed, reels, stories, groups, marketplace, and other; Instagram that brings people closer through Instag...
overview.financialIntro: In 2025, Meta Platforms's revenue was $200.97 billion, an increase of 22.17% compared to the previous year's $164.50 billion. Earnings were $60.46 billion, a decrease of -3.05%.
overview.analystIntro: According to 63 analysts, the average rating for META stock is "Strong Buy." The 12-month stock price target is $822.69, which is an increase of 27.35% from the latest price.

data.title: Can the ‘Magnificent Seven' save a stock market that might be doomed without them?
data.text: Apple, Amazon, Nvidia and peers are quietly coming back to life, and that could easily jolt this sleepy stock market back into gear.

data.text: Meta is rolling out a new safety feature that will see parents alerted if their teenager discusses suicide or self-harm with Instagram's built-in AI chatbot.

data.title: Meta Oversight Board finds top AI models less likely to criticize repressive regimes
data.text: AI models from leading labs including Anthropic and OpenAI are much less likely to criticize governments known ​for restricting free speech, Meta's Oversight Board said ‌on Thursday.

data.title: Companies laying off staff this year include Meta, Amazon, and Groupon — see the list
data.text: Companies including Meta, Amazon, and Walmart have said they're trimming staff this year. Many firms, like Wix, have cited the rise of AI as a reason they need fewer staff.

data.title: Meta employees allege discriminatory AI-driven layoffs
data.text: Twenty-six Meta employees have filed a lawsuit accusing the tech giant of using artificial intelligence to select workers for mass layoffs, a claim strongly denied by the trillion-dollar company.

data.title: Meta Workers Accuse It of Using AI to Conduct Discriminatory Layoffs
data.text: A lawsuit alleged the company relied on AI that disproportionately targeted workers with disabilities or who were on medical or parental leave.

data.title: Meta used AI to tag workers who took leave to be laid off, lawsuit claims
data.text: Lawsuit filed by dozens of employees says people who took maternity or disability leave were disproportionately selected for layoffs
